target.name: Icarus
target.founder: Henry Kwan (CEO, fmr Orbital — built spacecraft + space robots, helped win $935M NASA space habitat contract; fmr NASA flight sciences drone designer; M.S. Aerospace Engineering Georgia Tech; TS/SCI clearance)

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Icarus is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Henry Kwan, whose prior roles include spacecraft and space-robotics engineering at Orbital (including a $935M NASA space habitat contract win) and NASA drone design work. The target operates within a 10-peer comparable universe: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set density. The Defense & Security sector contains 19 total peers, but filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than three datapoints. A broad all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the sector's distinct cost-of-capital and growth-rate profile. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from the comparable-company method using the available data.

The analysis is constrained by the scarcity of seed-stage Defense & Security comps with sufficient financial disclosure. Any valuation must rely on alternative methods (DCF, venture-capital method, or asset-based approaches) not included in the current dataset. Kwan's operational track record and security clearance mitigate execution risk but do not substitute for market-based validation. Further data collection on the two public peers' financials and the five named private peers' most recent rounds would be necessary to support a defended range.
